title: Helios Install-Origin Schema and Generation

tests.name: Schema created
tests.detail: schemas/helios-install-origin.v1.json created with 13 required fields, 8 optional fields

tests.name: Parse validation - AkashicHeliosInstallPlan.ps1
tests.detail: Zero parse errors after Phase 10 insertion and phase renumbering (10-14 -> 11-15)

tests.name: Origin generation produces valid output
tests.detail: All 13 required schema fields present, all 8 optional fields populated

tests.name: Source protected hashes recorded
tests.detail: 5 source files hashed from RuntimeBundleRoot: helios_pretooluse.ps1, gate_check.ps1, evidence_capture.ps1, tier_classifier.ps1, command-policy.json

tests.name: Installed runtime hashes recorded
tests.detail: 6 installed files hashed from HeliosGateRoot: 5 source files + HeliosIntegrityBridge.ps1

tests.name: Bridge drift detection
tests.detail: Correctly detected bridge drift: AkashicIntegrityBridge (ba6c95...) != HeliosIntegrityBridge (c26f92...). origin_verified=false is correct for this state.

tests.name: Source/installed consistency check
tests.detail: All 5 source protected hashes match their installed counterparts (expected because source=installed in this test)

tests.name: Akashic installer tool hashes recorded
tests.detail: 6 installer tools hashed including New-HeliosInstallOrigin.ps1 itself

tests.name: Install plan integration
tests.detail: Phase 10 inserted after envelope verification (Phase 9), existing phases renumbered 11-15. Origin status added to evidence and plan assembly.

notes: origin_verified=false in the test run is correct and expected. The test used RuntimeBundleRoot=HeliosGateRoot, so all 5 source files matched. Bridge drift was correctly detected because the live HeliosIntegrityBridge.ps1 has drifted from the current AkashicIntegrityBridge.ps1. In a real install flow, bridge sync (Phase 6-7) runs before origin generation (Phase 10), so origin_verified would be true.
